whatHappening: Miami Food Pug is publishing its weekly Friday roundup of South Florida food news and events for May 22, 2026. The compilation features food-related stories and local happenings that didn't make it into earlier coverage during the week.
whoInvolved: Miami Food Pug is a South Florida-based food media outlet that curates and covers the regional dining and culinary scene. The publication serves as a local news aggregator for food enthusiasts and industry followers in the Miami area.
whyMatters: This weekly roundup keeps South Florida's food community informed about emerging trends, restaurant openings, and culinary events happening across the region. For food businesses and consumers alike, this coverage provides visibility and connectivity within the local dining ecosystem.
moreContext: Miami Food Pug established a regular Friday feature to capture food-related news that accumulates throughout the week but lacks enough depth for standalone coverage. South Florida's diverse food scene—influenced by Caribbean, Latin American, and contemporary American cuisines—generates significant weekly news and event activity that benefits from this type of curated roundup.

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour is emerging as Miami Beach's premier alternative to the tourist-saturated Ocean Drive scene. The neighborhood is positioning itself as a wellness-focused, walkable destination that prioritizes local lifestyle over chaotic tourism.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated, a local lifestyle publication, is highlighting Sunset Harbour's unique positioning. The neighborhood itself comprises local businesses, residents, and wellness-oriented establishments catering to a more discerning demographic than typical South Beach venues.
whyMatters: This shift reflects broader Miami Beach tourism trends toward curated, experiential destinations rather than mass-market party scenes. The development of Sunset Harbour as a lifestyle hub could reshape how Miami attracts visitors and residents seeking authentic, health-conscious urban experiences.
moreContext: Sunset Harbour's bay-side location on the quieter west side of South Beach provides geographic separation from Ocean Drive's high-energy nightlife district. The neighborhood's evolution toward wellness and local culture represents a countermovement to South Beach's traditional party reputation. As Miami continues attracting remote workers and affluent relocators, neighborhoods like Sunset Harbour offer alternatives that appeal to demographics seeking walkability, dining, and community engagement. This positioning also reflects Miami's broader rebranding efforts to attract quality tourism and permanent residents beyond its spring-break image.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants
summary: The City of Coral Gables’ popular restaurant month, Taste the Gables, returns this July with its most expansive lineup to date. More than 75 restaurants, ranging from Michelin-recognized destinations to neighborhood favorites and varied cuisines, are participating, showcasing…
whatHappening: Taste the Gables, Coral Gables' signature restaurant month, is returning this July with 75 participating establishments—the largest roster in the event's history. Diners can experience offerings from Michelin-recognized fine dining to casual neighborhood spots across multiple cuisines.
whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the month-long culinary event, partnering with restaurants ranging from upscale destinations to local favorites throughout the municipality.
whyMatters: The expanded event drives foot traffic and revenue to local restaurants during the summer season, traditionally a slower period for dining establishments. It also positions Coral Gables as a premier dining destination and strengthens the community's restaurant economy through increased visibility and customer engagement.
moreContext: Taste the Gables has become an annual fixture that celebrates the city's diverse culinary scene. The inclusion of Michelin-recognized venues alongside neighborhood staples indicates the breadth of dining options available in Coral Gables. Restaurant promotion events like this are particularly valuable during summer months when tourism and local dining typically decline. The record participation of 75 restaurants suggests growing merchant interest and community appetite for these curated dining experiences.

title: New Restaurant Reviews + Eating News – Part 2
summary: More new restaurant reviews, vinyl nights, Persian happy hour with delish light bites, restaurant closings and more in Part 2 of eating news in June. For Part 1, click here.
whatHappening: Miami's restaurant scene is actively shifting with new openings, themed dining events, and closures documented in the second installment of June eating news. Venues are launching vinyl nights and Persian happy hour specials while some establishments are shuttering.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated, a local food and lifestyle publication, is curating coverage of independent and emerging restaurants across Miami's diverse dining landscape. The reporting captures activity from various restaurant operators and venue owners introducing new concepts and events to the market.
whyMatters: These updates reflect the real-time dynamics of Miami's competitive restaurant market, where venues must continually innovate with themed events and happy hour offerings to attract diners. The mix of openings and closings signals which dining concepts resonate with Miami consumers and how the market is consolidating around successful formats.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant scene has increasingly adopted experiential dining—combining food with entertainment like vinyl nights to differentiate from competitors. The inclusion of ethnic cuisines like Persian with specialized happy hours reflects Miami's diverse demographics and growing consumer interest in global culinary experiences. Restaurant closures are ongoing post-pandemic as the market stabilizes and weaker concepts exit. Part 2 of this coverage series indicates Miami Curated is providing ongoing monthly tracking of the local dining landscape, establishing itself as a resource for residents monitoring restaurant trends.

title: New Restaurant Reviews + More Eating News: Part 1
summary: Reviews of new restaurant openings including Casa Tua Cucina Wynwood, "Meet me at Milos "spritz and aperitivo special, a Dubai chewy cookie, new top spot for coffee lovers with a Norwegian dessert, first Cuban restaurant to get a Michelin star and a bundle for non alcoholic…
whatHappening: Miami's dining scene is experiencing a wave of new restaurant openings and notable culinary achievements. The city is welcoming diverse dining concepts ranging from Italian cuisine to aperitivo bars, specialty coffee shops, and historically significant Michelin-starred establishments.
whoInvolved: Multiple new restaurants are launching in Miami, including Casa Tua Cucina in Wynwood, a venue called Milos specializing in spritz and aperitivo offerings, and notably, the first Cuban restaurant to earn a Michelin star. These establishments represent both local entrepreneurs and established culinary brands expanding into the market.
whyMatters: These openings reflect Miami's growing status as a destination for serious dining and culinary innovation, attracting both local diners and tourists seeking diverse experiences. The Michelin star awarded to a Cuban restaurant is particularly significant as it represents recognition of traditional Cuban cuisine at the highest culinary levels, elevating the cultural profile of the city's food scene.
moreContext: Miami has been positioning itself as a major food destination in recent years, with increased focus on chef-driven restaurants and diverse international cuisines. The aperitivo bar trend reflects growing consumer interest in European-style social drinking culture with food pairings. The inclusion of specialty coffee shops with Norwegian desserts and non-alcoholic beverage options indicates restaurants are catering to broader dietary preferences and occasions beyond traditional full-service dining, reflecting post-pandemic consumer behavior shifts toward flexibility and variety.

title: A Traditional Italian Fave Made Even Better: Il Mulino
summary: Il Mulino New York located at Acqualina Resort, the Five Star beachfront property which recently celebrated its 20th anniversary, has always been one of my favorite traditional Italian restaurants in Miami. It’s authentic, gives good quality/value, the food is reliably very…
whatHappening: Il Mulino New York, a traditional Italian restaurant located at the Acqualina Resort in Miami, is being highlighted as a standout dining destination. The restaurant continues to deliver authentic Italian cuisine with strong quality-to-value offerings at Miami's premier beachfront property.
whoInvolved: Il Mulino New York operates at Acqualina Resort, a Five Star beachfront property in Miami that recently marked its 20th anniversary. The restaurant is part of the acclaimed Il Mulino brand, known for authentic Italian dining experiences.
whyMatters: In Miami's competitive restaurant scene, Il Mulino represents a reliable option for diners seeking genuine Italian cuisine with consistent quality. The restaurant's presence at a luxury resort property elevates the destination's culinary reputation and appeals to both locals and high-end tourists.
moreContext: Acqualina Resort celebrated its 20th anniversary recently, establishing itself as a longstanding luxury beachfront destination in Miami. Il Mulino has maintained a strong reputation as one of Miami's preferred traditional Italian restaurants, distinguishing itself through authenticity and value proposition. The dining venue benefits from its location within a Five Star property, attracting affluent clientele and resort guests. The restaurant's enduring popularity suggests strong demand for high-quality Italian dining experiences in the Miami market.

title: Miami’s New Modern Mexican Hotspot—What You Need to Know
summary: We went to the Mexican Miami less than three weeks after opening and it was buzzy and many of its seats taken with individuals at the bar, small groups at high top round tables, and others headed to the private rooms celebrating a special occasion. The word has gotten out…
whatHappening: A new Modern Mexican restaurant has opened in Miami and is already generating significant buzz just three weeks after launch. The venue is drawing crowds across all dining formats—bar seating, high-top tables, and private dining areas.
whoInvolved: Mexican Miami is the restaurant in question, operating in the competitive Miami dining scene. The venue has attracted attention from Miami Curated, a local food and lifestyle publication covering the city's restaurant landscape.
whyMatters: The restaurant's rapid popularity suggests strong market demand for elevated Mexican cuisine in Miami. Early success indicates potential for sustained business growth and could influence other restaurateurs' investment decisions in the Modern Mexican category.
moreContext: Miami's dining scene has seen growing interest in upscale interpretations of traditional cuisines, with Modern Mexican becoming an increasingly trendy category. The restaurant's diverse seating options—casual bar, social high-tops, and private event spaces—indicate a strategy to capture multiple revenue streams and dining occasions. The fact that the venue was already at significant capacity within three weeks of opening suggests effective marketing, word-of-mouth momentum, or pent-up demand for this particular concept in the market.
